job_title: View job: Scientist II, Clinical Bioinformatics
job_description:
• Hands-on experience with 10x Genomics single-cell and in-situ transcriptomics technologies is a strong preference Hands-on research experience in cancer or autoimmune diseases is a strong preference Knowledge of clinical genomics, biomarker discovery and diagnostics Development of statistical models and algorithms for single-cell or spatial transcriptomics data Application of machine learning, particularly in the context of genomics Proficiency with workflow orchestration frameworks such as Snakemake, Nextflow or Martian Programming best practices including data analysis reproducibility, version control, design patterns, testing, debugging and profiling Track record of writing production-level code or maintaining published software packages High-throughput computing infrastructure such as HPCs or cloud computing Ph.
• In bioinformatics, computational biology, genomics or a related discipline with extensive hands-on experience in single-cell NGS data analysis.
• A minimum of 2 years of industry experience post Ph.
• Experience analyzing large-scale single-cell or spatial transcriptomics datasets to derive biologically meaningful insights and/or diagnostic biomarkers.
• Experience developing and optimizing high-performance, scalable code.
• In-depth understanding of the assumptions, limitations and caveats of statistical methods.
• Proficiency working in a Linux environment.
• Goal-oriented, self-motivated and an independent problem solver.
